Major Periods.description: Widespread bronze metallurgy, first cities and states.
Widespread iron metallurgy, collapse of Bronze Age civilizations.
From the invention of writing to the fall of Rome.
Spread of Greek culture from the Mediterranean to Central Asia after Alexander.
From the fall of Rome to the discovery of America.
Dominant social and political organization of medieval Europe, based on bonds of vassalage.
Cultural, artistic and intellectual renewal in Europe, from Italy across the continent.
From the Renaissance to the Enlightenment.
Voltaire, Rousseau, the Encyclopédie; challenge to religious and political dogmas.
From the French Revolution to today.

Europe & Mediterranean.description: Advanced culture in Crete with maritime trade.
Formation of Greek city-states and first Olympic Games.
Height of Greek culture, philosophy and science.
Rapid conquest of Mediterranean Asia as far as India.
Rome expands across the Mediterranean before becoming an empire.
Great Mediterranean power influential in culture, law and administration.
Great migration and warrior empire in Eurasia.
Germanic people settled in Spain and France.
Germanic people in North Africa after the fall of Rome.
Continuation of the Roman Empire at Constantinople.
European expansion led by Napoleon Bonaparte.
Largest empire in modern history, covering up to a quarter of the world's land area.

Africa.description: Rise of the pharaonic state and construction of the great pyramids.
Powerful Nubian kingdom south of Egypt, rival and occasional conqueror of the pharaohs.
Powerful trading state and rival of Rome in North Africa.
Prosperous African empire through trade in gold and salt.
Powerful Christian Ethiopian empire trading with Arabia.
West African empire famous for Mansa Musa and Timbuktu.

Middle East & Asia.description: First city-states, organized agriculture and cuneiform writing.
Advanced urban civilization (Mohenjo-daro, Harappa), one of the oldest in the world.

Middle East & Asia.titre: Babylon and the Code of Hammurabi

Middle East & Asia.description: Empire of Anatolia, known for its military influence.
First documented Chinese dynasty with writing.
Powerful military empire dominating the ancient Near East.
Nebuchadnezzar II, Hanging Gardens of Babylon, deportation of the Hebrews.
Great empire stretching from India to the Mediterranean.
First great Indian empire, height under Ashoka and spread of Buddhism.
Chinese golden age, trade via the Silk Road.
Great rival of Rome then Byzantium, heir to Persian culture.
Rapid expansion of Islam across Africa, Asia and Europe.
Golden age of medieval China, cultural and commercial openness.
Period of great prosperity and technical innovations.
Military rule of the shoguns, era of the samurai and gradual isolation of Japan.
Great Islamic empire of South Asia, height under Akbar and Shah Jahan (Taj Mahal).

Americas.description: Great Mesoamerican civilization with writing and astronomy.
Great Mesoamerican civilization at its height before the Spanish conquest.
Great Andean empire of South America.

Conflicts & Conquests.description: Christian military expeditions to the Holy Land and the Mediterranean.
Progressive Christian reconquest of the Iberian Peninsula from Muslim kingdoms.
Explorations and conquests in America, Africa, Asia and Oceania.

Conflicts & Conquests.titre: Transatlantic Slave Trade and Colonial Slavery
Conflicts & Conquests.description: Massive deportation of Africans to the Americas via the triangular trade; progressive abolition (UK 1833, France 1848, USA 1865, Brazil 1888).
Division of the world among European powers, race for colonies in Africa and Asia.
Major world conflict with redefinition of alliances.
Independence of colonized peoples in Africa, Asia and Oceania.
Ideological confrontation between East and West blocs.
Technological competition between the United States and the USSR, from Sputnik to Apollo and beyond.

Pandemics.description: First major pandemic in recorded history.
Medieval pandemic that killed a third of Europe.
Global spread especially after European contact with the Americas.
Six successive global pandemics originating in South Asia, transforming public hygiene.
Global pandemic following World War I.
Global pandemic of the SARS-CoV-2 virus.

Culture, Ideas & Turning Points.description: Appearance of the first writing systems in Mesopotamia and Egypt.
Network of trade and cultural routes linking China to the Mediterranean.
Luther, Calvin and the fracture of the Catholic Church; wars of religion in Europe.
From Copernicus to Newton, overturning the worldview and birth of modern sciences.
Challenge to monarchical order, birth of modern democracies.
Mechanization of production, massive urbanization and transformation of societies.
From the ECSC to the Treaty of Rome and the European Union, progressive integration of European nations.
Rise of computing, the internet and digital technologies transforming societies and economies.
Global integration of trade, technologies and cultures after the end of the Cold War.

Religions & Spiritualities.description: Oldest living religious tradition — from the Vedas to the Upanishads and the Bhagavad Gita; over one billion followers today.
Monotheism of the Hebrew people — Torah, prophets, Talmud; foundation of the Abrahamic religions.
Dualistic religion of Persia (Ahura Mazda vs Angra Mainyu) — decisive influence on Judaism, Christianity and Islam.
Founded by Siddhartha Gautama — the Four Noble Truths and the Eightfold Path; spread from India across Asia.
Founding philosophical and religious currents of Chinese civilization — social ethics (Confucius) and natural harmony (Laozi).
Founded on the life and teachings of Jesus of Nazareth — New Testament, Trinity, Church; the world's most widespread religion.
Founded by the prophet Muhammad in Mecca — the Quran, the five pillars, rapid expansion from Arabia to Asia, Africa and Europe.
Founded by Guru Nanak in Punjab (India) — egalitarian monotheism, rejection of the caste system, service to the community.

Science, Art & Culture.description: Beginning of cuneiform writing, allowing history to be recorded.

Science, Art & Culture.titre: Construction of the Great Pyramid of Khufu
Science, Art & Culture.description: Completion of the pyramid at Giza, Egypt.
Condemned to death by Athens, symbol of philosophical martyrdom.
Founding of the first university in Europe.
Universal Renaissance genius, artist and scientist.
Beginning of the mass dissemination of knowledge.
Copernican revolution — the Earth revolves around the Sun.
Foundations of classical mechanics and the law of universal gravitation.
Founding work of modern political philosophy and democracies.
Improvement of the steam engine, driving force of industrialization.

Science, Art & Culture.titre: Publication of Darwin's On the Origin of Species
Science, Art & Culture.description: Foundation of the theory of evolution by natural selection.
Neil Armstrong walks on the Moon.

Politics & Empires.description: Hammurabi becomes king of Babylon and codifies his laws.
According to tradition, Romulus founds Rome.
Cleisthenes' reforms establishing the foundations of democracy in Athens.
Beginning of the Roman civil war, Caesar marches on Rome.
Julius Caesar is assassinated by Roman senators.

Politics & Empires.titre: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
Politics & Empires.description: End of the Western Roman Empire, beginning of the Middle Ages.
Charlemagne becomes emperor of the Holy Roman Empire.
Unification of the Mongol tribes and beginning of the Mongol Empire.
First charter of liberties signed in England, limiting royal power.
The Byzantine Empire falls to the Ottomans, end of the Middle Ages.

Politics & Empires.titre: Execution of Mary Queen of Scots
Politics & Empires.description: Mary Queen of Scots is beheaded in England.
King of France assassinated by François Ravaillac.
End of the Thirty Years' War, birth of the modern nation-state system.
King of England beheaded during the English Revolution.
Establishment of constitutional monarchy, limits on royal power.
Official proclamation of American independence.
Overthrow of the absolute monarchy, proclamation of human rights and birth of the French Republic.
Seizure of power by Lenin and the Bolsheviks, birth of the USSR.
Founding of the United Nations to maintain world peace.
End of the division of Germany and symbol of the end of the Cold War.
End of the Soviet Union, collapse of the Eastern Bloc and end of the Cold War.

Wars & Battles.description: First peace treaty in history, signed between Ramesses II and the Hittites.
Greek victory against the Persians, symbol of democratic resistance.
Beginning of the expedition into Asia, construction of the greatest empire of Antiquity.
Conflicts between Rome and Carthage for dominance over the Mediterranean.
Charles Martel halts the Muslim expansion into Western Europe.
Norman conquest of England by William the Conqueror.
Christian expedition to the Holy Land.

Wars & Battles.titre: Beginning of the Hundred Years' War
Wars & Battles.description: Long conflict between France and England for the French throne.
Burned at the stake in Rouen, heroine of the Hundred Years' War.

Wars & Battles.titre: Beginning of the Thirty Years' War
Wars & Battles.description: Devastating religious and political conflict in Central Europe.

Wars & Battles.titre: Beginning of the Seven Years' War
Wars & Battles.description: First conflict of global scale involving the major European powers.
Armistice of 11 November 1918, end of the deadliest conflict in history to that date.

Wars & Battles.titre: Nuclear Bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
Wars & Battles.description: First use of nuclear weapons in warfare.
Terrorist attacks in the United States, beginning of the War on Terror.
War in Europe, the largest military offensive since World War II.

Religion & Spirituality.description: Sacred founding texts of Hinduism, composed in Sanskrit in the Ganges plain — the oldest living religious tradition.

Religion & Spirituality.titre: Reign of David — United Monarchy of Israel
Religion & Spirituality.description: Unification of the Hebrew tribes under David, founder of Jerusalem as capital; foundations of Judaism.
The prophet Zarathustra founds Zoroastrianism in Persia — the first great dualistic monotheism, spiritual ancestor of the Abrahamic religions.
Siddhartha Gautama, founder of Buddhism, born in India.
Chinese philosopher and founder of Confucianism — social ethics, filial piety, virtuous governance.
Death and resurrection of Jesus of Nazareth — theological foundation of Christianity.

Religion & Spirituality.titre: Edict of Milan — Legalization of Christianity
Religion & Spirituality.description: Constantine and Licinius grant freedom of worship in the Roman Empire; major turning point for Christianity.

Religion & Spirituality.titre: Edict of Thessalonica — Christianity as State Religion
Religion & Spirituality.description: Theodosius I makes Nicene Christianity the official religion of the Roman Empire.
Founder of Islam, born in Mecca.
Migration of Muhammad from Mecca to Medina, beginning of the Islamic calendar.
Final separation between the Roman Catholic Church and the Eastern Orthodox Church.

Religion & Spirituality.titre: Birth of Guru Nanak — Founding of Sikhism
Religion & Spirituality.description: Guru Nanak born in Punjab; his teachings found Sikhism, an egalitarian monotheism rejecting the caste system.
Posting of the 95 Theses at Wittenberg.

Pandemics & Crises.titre: Beginning of the Black Death in Europe
Pandemics & Crises.description: Bubonic plague pandemic that killed a third of the European population.

Explorations & Conquests.titre: Conquest of the Inca Empire by Pizarro
First Permanent English Colony in America
Explorations & Conquests.description: Founding of Jamestown in Virginia, beginning of British colonization.
